title: [Feature]: Add durable replay-to-sinks guidance and tooling design
problem: Users may hear 'ordered consumer' and assume it is the right tool for replaying historical events into production sinks. That is unsafe for nats-sinks because production sink writes require durable pull-consumer semantics, explicit ACK behavior, idempotency, and controlled replay boundaries.
proposal: Add a dedicated durable replay-to-sinks design that explains how to replay to Oracle, file, and future sinks using durable pull consumers, explicit start sequence or time policies, idempotent sink keys, bounded batches, and dry-run validation. This work should be separate from ordered inspection tooling and should avoid using ordered consumers for production sink writes.
users: Operators planning controlled reprocessing, incident-response teams rebuilding downstream state, and maintainers designing replay tooling for production sinks.
delivery_semantics: Replay-to-sinks must use commit-then-acknowledge and idempotent sink writes. A replay tool must never ACK before durable sink success and must never claim that ordered inspection consumers provide durable replay semantics.
security: Replay can duplicate or expose sensitive operational data. The design must require explicit non-production dry runs, redacted reports, bounded subject scope, least-privilege NATS permissions, and sink-specific idempotency review before production use.
acceptance: Documentation separates ordered inspection from durable replay-to-sinks.
The design uses durable pull consumers and commit-then-acknowledge for sink writes.
Replay boundaries such as start sequence, start time, subject scope, and max count are documented.
Dry-run and redacted report expectations are documented.
Tests or future test design cover no early ACK, idempotency, and bounded replay.
tests: Add design-level tests or future unit-test requirements for replay configuration validation, redacted report generation, no early ACK, and idempotent duplicate handling. Run scripts/check.sh when implemented.
documentation: Update durable replay, ordered-consumer evaluation, operations, testing, sink framework, roadmap, test-report, and changelog documentation.
closeout: Close after the release containing durable replay-to-sinks guidance or tooling design is published. Include release tag, docs, tests, and limitations.
